items.title: TennoCon 2026 is on the event horizon, and here's what to expect
items.summary: Soulframe and Warframe and teasers, oh my!
items.content:
When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Digital Extremes) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Get ready, Warframe and Soulframe devotees, because the annual Digital Extremes convention you do not want to miss is nearly here: TennoCon 2026 takes place in beautiful London, Ontario, for two entire days of events and activities – physically and virtually – on July 10 and July 11. Ahead of that, the road to TennoCon 2026 has now kicked off in earnest with its branding reveal alongside a new trailer hyping up everything to expect out of the convention.

As you can see in the trailer, there's plenty to be excited about from the TennoConcert to the TennoCon 2026 Relay Experience. While the official TennoCon 2026 website serves as your one-stop shop for all things TennoCon this year, there's actually quite a bit of specific detail to wrap your head around so you're not missing out on anything that's important to you.

Watch On The in-person and stream schedules for TennoCon 2026 are already live , but given tickets for the in-person event are sold out, it is perhaps most useful to understand exactly how you can virtually participate. That goes beyond simply watching the various panels as they are broadcast, though it doesn't hurt to keep those in mind if any catch your eye. Notably, actors Ben Starr and Jennifer English will make appearances on Warframe and Soulframe panels while in-person attendees may have the chance to meet other actors in attendance.

Specifically, the cosplay contest on Friday, July 10, at 7pm ET is followed by the TennoConcert 2026 at 8:30pm ET. If you're not physically attending at Canada Life Place, you can watch online as "13 years of soundscapes from across the Origin System" are conducted by Warframe composer Matthew Chalmers – and performed by a full orchestra.

Best of all? There will be Twitch Drops for the broadcast stream on both days of TennoCon, which you can see for yourself in the trailer. So long as you've successfully linked your Twitch and Warframe accounts , you can watch TennoCon 2026 programming via official Warframe Twitch and Steam channels to earn the following rewards:

Warframe codes (June 2026) and how to redeem them for boosters, glyphs, decorations, and more Summer Game Fest schedule 2026: Dates, times, and where to watch the showcases Summer Game Fest Live 2026 time – how to watch the stream Friday, July 10: Cosplay Contest + TennoConcert if (window.sliceHydrationLazy) { window.sliceHydrationLazy("imageGallery-8kHhTx5fCpmSTsiaWTvqaD-xNaPZKlBdstRSSfPbc8SnItBQwrUYTlk", "imageGallery", JSON.stringify({"galleryData":[{"title":"","description":[],"image":{"id":"rDJ4Vg8GqgxRpkkFAoY6Ug","name":"tennocon 2026 twitch drops 2","credit":{"text":"(Image credit: {subject})","subject":"Digital Extremes"},"src":"https:\/\/cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net\/rDJ4Vg8GqgxRpkkFAoY6Ug.png","alt":"TennoCon 2026 giveaway for July 10 banner","width":1920,"height":1080,"srcSetSizes":[320,480,650,970,1024,1200],"sizes":{"default":"calc(100vw - 40px)","1000px":"970px"},"fullscreen":false,"lazyLoading":true,"addSEOMetaData":false,"removeNativeWidthRestriction":false,"dataBordeauxImageCheckAttr":false,"noCredit":false}},{"title":"","description":[],"image":{"id":"B3uedYpsUnGnmFJJJXBsKg","name":"tennocon 2026 twitch drops 1","credit":{"text":"(Image credit: {subject})","subject":"Digital Extremes"},"src":"https:\/\/cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net\/B3uedYpsUnGnmFJJJXBsKg.png","alt":"TennoCon 2026 giveaway for July 10 banner","width":1920,"height":1080,"srcSetSizes":[320,480,650,970,1024,1200],"sizes":{"default":"calc(100vw - 40px)","1000px":"970px"},"fullscreen":false,"lazyLoading":true,"addSEOMetaData":false,"removeNativeWidthRestriction":false,"dataBordeauxImageCheckAttr":false,"noCredit":false}}],"progressText":"Image {currentSlide} of {totalSlides}","viewOriginalText":"View Original"}), "https://slice.vanilla.futurecdn.net/13-4-24/js/imageGallery.js"); } else { console.error('%c FTE ','background: #9306F9; color: #ffffff','no lazy slice hydration function available'); } Image 1 of 2 (Image credit: Digital Extremes) (Image credit: Digital Extremes) Drop 3: Haalvu (New Primary Weapon) - 11am ET - 4pm ET Drop 4: Mesa Prime - 4pm ET - 6pm ET There will also be physical giveaways at TennoCon 2026, including potential prizes like a complete Warframe Hyper X headset kit, Warframe-branded Jade City hot sauce, and in-game Platinum. More giveaways are expected to be announced prior to the event itself, so keep your eyes peeled.

And if you're not content to simply watch TennoCon 2026, you can also take part in the action. The TennoCon 2026 Relay Experience, which invites Warframe players to take part in a simultaneous in-game activity alongside TennoLive (the main event and gameplay presentation of the convention), begins on July 11 at 4pm ET. Exactly what to expect remains to be seen, with further teasers almost certainly set to release between now and TennoCon, but Digital Extremes often tries to go all out for the annual event with new demo showcases for both Warframe and Soulframe

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

There's certain to be even more announced ahead of TennoCon 2026, and who knows what the studio has in store. Between linking up Warframe and Twitch accounts if you're watching virtually or checking out the newly released showfloor maps if you're going to be there in person, there's more than enough to prepare for already.

In his spare time, Rollin likes to import Valkyria Chronicles merch and watch anime. items.title: Digital Extremes Prepares Millions of Players for Annual TennoCon Celebration
items.summary: Millions of 'Warframe' and 'Soulframe' fans prepare for Digital Extremes' event next month in London, Ontario.
items.content:
LONDON, Ontario – June 24, 2026 – TennoCon , the annual celebration of all things Warframe and Soulframe , is just weeks away. The record-breaking event hosted by Digital Extremes in its hometown of London, Ontario, unites millions of fans from around the world online, in-game, and in person, for a sold-out show featuring direct-to-player reveals and first-look demos of the next chapters of Warframe and Soulframe .

Everyone, everywhere, can enjoy the programming TennoCon offers for free, and all panels, reveals, giveaways, rewards, and merchandise are available whether attending in-person or tuning in from home. This year’s event features a premium lineup of physical and virtual experiences, including a Warframe concert , panels highlighting the crafts of game design, appearances and autographs from renowned actors, a Cosplay Contest , and so much more, all culminating in the finale TennoLive gameplay demo presentation.

“Our biggest TennoCon yet may be around the corner, but we have so much more in store for players this year,” said Megan Everett, Community Director. “Both Warframe and Soulframe have bustling and passionate communities we want to celebrate and honor. The last thing we want is for anyone to feel left out, and our team has pushed our creativity to the limit to create unforgettable TennoCon experiences that anyone can join, both in person and online! Our TennoCon Relay experience planned for TennoLive is one of the coolest experiences we’ve done to date, and I cannot stress enough how you do not want to miss it.”

Prepare for the biggest Warframe and Soulframe announcements of the year with the TennoCon 2026 countdown trailer here

In-Game TennoCon 2026 Relay Experience: An unassuming ship spaceport is the setting for this year’s in-game TennoCon Relay … but not all is as it seems for this genuinely once-in-a-lifetime synchronous in-game experience for the Warframe community. Celebrate TennoCon with fellow players around the world and share a moment in real time around TennoLive’s biggest reveals . The TennoCon 2026 Relay will be available in Warframe on Saturday, July 11, starting at 4:00 p.m. ET.

Drop into Exclusive Rewards on Twitch and Steam: Tune into the Cosplay Contest and TennoConcert on Friday, July 10, to earn the Verminia Cosplay Community Display (by community artist Luckygoblinnn ) and Singing Lotus Community Glyph (by community artist cyanfacade ). Watch mainstage TennoCon panels and the TennoLive demo presentations on Saturday, July 11, to earn the new primary weapon, Haalvu, and the Mesa Prime Warframe. Drops are available to live viewers on Twitch (link Twitch & Warframe accounts here ) and Steam. In-person attendees will receive a code via email for these drops to redeem at home.

For full details on how and where to earn livestream drops, check out the Giveaways & Extras page on the TennoCon website here.

No Tenno or Envoy Left Behind: Take part in TennoCon 2026 either in-person in London, Ontario, or virtually from anywhere in the world via Warframe’s ( Twitch / YouTube ) and Soulframe’s ( Twitch / YouTube ) broadcast channels. Create your own event badge at home and tune into TennoCon programming for the return of the Cosplay Contest and TennoConcert on July 10, celebrating 13 years of iconic Warframe soundtracks with a symphony conducted by Matthew Chalmers. On July 11, engage in unique panels featuring special guests alongside Digital Extremes’ global community of millions in the lead-up to the event’s finale gameplay demo presentation, TennoLive.

Players attending TennoCon in-person can prepare for the event using the newly available complete schedule and show floor maps. Activities include autograph signings, exclusive merch, photo opportunities, an outdoor Soulframe experience, and more. Attendees can also expect to see familiar Warframe faces such as The Devil’s Triad’s René Zagger (Roathe), Will de-Renzy Martin (Lyon), and Elsa Pérusin (Marie) from The Old Peace , as well as fan-favorite characters Ben Starr (Arthur), and Kevin Lim (Ordis). Soulframe’s voice talent will also be attending for the first time, featuring BAFTA-award-winning actor Jennifer English (Empress of Eldveil), Kim Bonifay (Verminia), Dan Pye (Avakot), as well as Ben Starr, commemorating his new role as Tempest Bayor.

To view the full virtual programming, in-person activity schedule, roster of guests and partners, including Arborealkey , Hoku Props , Blink Cosplay , and Plexi Cosplay , or the convention map, visit the Schedule & Maps page on the TennoCon website here.

Giveaways On the Road to TennoCon: Ramp up for the excitement early with various giveaways available through July 17.

Additional giveaways from OMEN GAMING HUB, Champion, and Madrinas are still to come. For more information, the Terms & Conditions of all giveaways can be found on the Warframe website here .

Limited-Time TennoCon Goodies: For those who want to continue celebrating TennoCon season in-game before or after the show, the limited-time TennoCon 2026 Digital Pack , including exclusive cosmetics and currency, is still available for purchase on the Warframe Market. Players looking to expand their collections can purchase the latest TennoCon Merch Pack for a commemorative t-shirt, event pin, the Loiaus Medallion, and more.

For more information on TennoCon 2026, please visit the official event website , visit the new official Warframe wiki , or follow our Bluesky , Twitter/X , Instagram , YouTube , Twitch , and TikTok accounts.

items.title: Two Weeks to TennoCon 2026!
items.summary: It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11. Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Pla
items.content:
It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11.

Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Place, complete with a live orchestra conducted by Warframe composer Matthew Chalmers. Then on July 11, we will be broadcasting live from Canada Life Place again for an entire day of panels, community activities and so much more before everything culminates in TennoLive, where secrets that many thought were lost to time will be laid bare — shaking the Origin System to its very core. Prepare yourself accordingly, Tenno.

Watch for Free Rewards

It wouldn’t be TennoCon without plenty of free Rewards on Twitch and Steam that you can earn just by watching! Be sure to link your Warframe and Twitch accounts ahead of time. Starting on July 10, watch the Cosplay Contest from 7 p.m. ET - 8:30 p.m. ET and TennoConcert from 8:30 p.m. ET to 10 p.m. ET for 30 minutes to earn the following, respectively: - Verminia Cosplay Community Display by Community Creator Luckygoblinnn - Singing Lotus Community Glyph (by Community Creator Cyanfacade) + Built Forma Then on July 11, watch any part of TennoCon from 11 a.m. ET - 4:30 p.m. ET and TennoLive from 4:30 p.m. ET to 6 p.m. ET for 30 minutes each to earn the following: - Haalvu (Primary Weapon) - Mesa Prime Please note: those attending the show in-person with a valid ticket will receive Twitch Drop items via promo codes through an email after TennoCon.

Digital Pack

Further enhance your Arsenal with the TennoCon 2026 Digital Pack, featuring the Hunhullus Ephemera, TennoCon2026 Emote and plenty more!

BUY NOW

Whether you’re watching from home or joining us in-person, we can’t wait to experience TennoCon 2026 with you all and reveal a bold new future for Warframe!

items.title: "Truthfully, it's heartbreaking": Warframe dev on the hole that Destiny 2 leaves behind
items.summary: "A game is healthy when you have competitors."
items.content:
News by Oisin Kuhnke Contributor Published on June 23, 2026 3 comments Follow Destiny 2 There's long been a bit of a rivalry between Destiny and Warframe 's respective communities because of them both being shooty, sci-fi, MMOy joints. But when it comes to the community director behind Warframe, the recent end to new Destiny 2 updates and expansions is no victory, and mostly just finds the whole situation to be "devastating."

Speaking to Eurogamer , community director and live ops lead Megan Everett shared that the Destiny 2 news is "heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down." Everett noted nobody is "celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story," and spoke of how even though the two games have been spoken of as in competition, she believes that "a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [Destiny's developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in."

Everett made specific mention of how one of the saddest elements was how following the end of support announcement, many came out of the woodworks to show support after a long period of negativity. "They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love! People need to put the hate away and show the love, because they could have really used that. I know it was there, but it wasn't as vocal as maybe some of the negativity."

Looping back to those Destiny and Warframe comparisons, Everett also made one thing clear for those looking to switch games: "We tried the PvP thing, and we're not a PvP game! If you're coming to Warframe trying to scratch that itch, you're going to have that itch for a long time." At the very least, Destiny 2's servers will live on, so you'll at least have somewhere to scratch that itch if you need to.

items.title: Warframe Devs Talk The Downfall Of Destiny 2: 'No One Is Celebrating'
items.summary: In a new interview with Eurogamer
items.content:
Joseph Bradford Posted: Jun 23, 2026 2:00 PM ET Category: News 0

In a new interview with Eurogamer's Connor Makar , Warframe's Megan Everett says that 'No one is celebrating' the downfall of Destiny 2, one of the sci-fi shooter's biggest rivals on the gaming stage.

Destiny 2 had its last ever content update earlier this month , bringing the narrative Bungie has been telling in its sci-fi universe for over a decade to a close. It was a sudden end for many of its fans as Bungie shifted resources to its new live-service shooter, Marathon , as well as new projects internally, and it's brought a depressing finality to one of the more interesting shooters on the market.

One game that, understandably, stands to benefit is Warframe , Digital Extremes' multiplayer shooter, and there is a ton of crossover between the fanbases and game experiences. Yet, as DE's community director and live ops lead, Megan Everett tells it, no one at the studio is celebrating a rival's demise.

Speaking to Eurogamer, Everett calls Bungie's shift of Destiny 2 into essentially maintenance mode "heartbreaking" and "earth-shattering." She continued saying that their game is better when there are competitors.

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [Destiny's developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in."

I think gamers oftentimes see the demise of one game as good news for another, and I'm inclined to agree with Everett here. Destiny 2 was made better because of the push that Warframe gave it, and vice versa. They had similarities but also served two different types of players, as Everett alludes to later in the interview, noting Warframe's general lack of PvP while Destiny 2 thrived on a healthy PvP environment.

Regardless of your thoughts on the Destiny franchise, or Bungie overall, it's been one of the most influential games over the series' decade-plus in operation, something that Warframe's Rebecca Ford pointed to back when the news that Destiny 2 was ending active development.

At the age of 16, me and my best friend (who gave me the name 'rebulast'), picked up our midnight release editions of Halo 3, sped home, told our parents it was important, and played more Halo than you could imagine.There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games.

You can check out the full interview on Eurogamer. If you're a Destiny player looking for your next home, our sister site, Overframe, has a case for trying Warframe .

Joseph has been writing or podcasting about games in some form since about 2012. Having written for multiple major outlets such as IGN, Playboy, and more, Joseph started writing for MMORPG in 2015. When he's not writing or talking about games, you can typically find him hanging out with his 15-year old or playing Magic: The Gathering with his family. Also, don't get him started on why Balrogs *don't* have wings. You can find him on Twitter @LotrLore

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch
items.summary: As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bri
items.content:
As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bring you a first-look at Sirius & Orion with a high-octane animated short worthy of the Stalker's enduring legacy. In honor of that incredible artistic collaboration and the dueling rivals at the center of our latest major update, we're pleased to bring you an exclusive, limited run of Warframe x THE LINE // Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch:

Warframe x THE LINE: Sirius T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-shirt imbues its wearer with the focused precision of Sirius. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Orion T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-Shirt imbues its wearer with the relentless determination of Orion. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Celestial Clash Poster

Two unstoppable warriors. Face-to-face (and scythe-to-scythe) clashing high above the wastes of the Orb Vallis. Now immortalized in an awe-inspiring, screenprinted 18" x 24" wall poster.

Warframe x THE LINE Merch Collection

Find harmony in the chaos of Sirius & Orion's eternal battle for the future when you bring home all three items from our latest line of merchandise. Includes both boxy, relaxed fit T-shirts honoring the dueling rivals, as well as their showstopping wall poster.

Special Re-release: Stalker Noggle

The Stalker returns once more in his most adorable physical form! Own the Stalker Noggle and add some charming malevolence to your IRL Base of Operations. Made from polyresin. Box size/Dimensions: 12cm x 12cm x 24cm.

BUY NOW

Please note: Quantities are limited. Get yours while they last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue Available for Pre-Order Now!

The Infestation is spreading beyond the Origin System. The ever-evolving Nidus is getting his very own collectible statue, complete with a commemorative coin and exclusive in-game bonuses. Pre-order yours now from the Warframe Store while quantities last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue

Bring the Infestation’s evolution to your collection with this highly detailed Nidus Statue. Standing at approximately 8.5” (21.59 cm) tall and crafted from polyresin, this collectible captures the grotesque power of Nidus in stunning detail. This purchase also includes an exclusive Nidus Coin crafted from Zinc Alloy at 1.75” (4 cm), an in-game Nidus Noggle and 170 Platinum!

BUY NOW

The Infestation waits for no one — secure your Nidus Collector’s Statue and Coin now!

items.title: Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors'
items.content:
When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Destiny 2 is slowly closing its doors—its servers aren't shutting down, but Bungie has emphasised that it won't be getting any more content, bar a massive quality of life patch that has left it in a relatively good, but inert and unchanging state.

One might, if they were being cynical and cutthroat, think this is cause for celebration—particularly if they were in charge of the competition, as Warframe is. Not so, says community director and live ops lead Megan Everett in a conversation with Eurogamer .

"Truthfully, it's heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down.

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors."

As someone in the MMO space, it's honestly true— World of Warcraft got its act together (at least, when compared to the catastrophe of Shadowlands ) when a mass exodus of fans drifted to Final Fantasy 14 . Conversely, with FF14's stagnation, it's starting to adopt a lot of the measures WoW and other MMOs did with Evercold, shaking things up .

'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable' Shock, tears, and relief: How Destiny 2's most popular creators reacted to the end of the legendary shooter Destiny 2 is dead, long live Destiny 3? 'Until I see the words Destiny 3 will never happen, I'm going to believe' My point being, healthy competition tends to drive games to be better than one another—and stop me if this is going off the reservation—but I think we can all broadly agree that we like it when videogames are good.

Everett also says it's a shame that Destiny 2's been shown support much too late: "I think what's unfortunate and sad is they announced this was happening, and suddenly people are showing all this support for them. They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love!

Keep up to date with the most important stories and the best deals, as picked by the PC Gamer team.

"Overall, I hate to see that happen to them. I have close friends who play Destiny over Warframe, and ain't mad about it. We have friends over there, to see this happen to them is devastating to the industry. No one saw Destiny going away"

He made his start as a freelancer, writing for websites like Techradar, The Escapist, Dicebreaker, The Gamer, Into the Spine\u2014and of course, PC Gamer. He'll sink his teeth into anything that looks interesting, though he has a soft spot for RPGs, soulslikes, roguelikes, deckbuilders, MMOs, and weird indie titles. He also plays a shelf load of TTRPGs in his offline time. Don't ask him what his favourite system is, he has too many. items.title: 'No One Is Celebrating' — Warframe Dev Calls End of Destiny 2 Support 'Earth-Shattering'
items.summary: Warframe community director Megan Everett has said that "no one is celebrating" the "heartbreaking" news that Sony pulled the plug on Destiny 2 support.
items.content:
Warframe community director and live ops lead Megan Everett has said that "no one is celebrating" the "heartbreaking" news that Sony pulled the plug on Destiny 2 support.

Both sci-fi shooters have had just enough in common to keep them in the same conversation for more than a decade. It's a friendly competition that carried developers Digital Extremes and Bungie from the 2010s into the 2020s but, when the latter announced it had shuttered all plans for future updates in May , both communities largely decided to lay down arms .

As Destiny players come to grips with the end of what has evolved into a go-to video game for thousands, some members of the Warframe team have chimed in to bid them farewell. Everett elaborated on her feelings during a conversation with Eurogamer .

"Truthfully, it's heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering," she said. "I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down."

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [Destiny's developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in."

The end of Destiny 2 came as a shock to everyone – Bungie included. After the announcement that support was winding down, Forbes reported that much of the studio's staff was unaware of plans to cease support until the public announcement . It means development on more Destiny content had continued even after the decision was made. The final update, Monument of Triumph , was launched on June 9.

It's been a tough pill to swallow, and one that's resulted in Destiny fans signing petitions and flooding livestream chats , seemingly to no avail. Everett is one of many who saw fans flock to Destiny 2's aid in its final days but, for her, it's hard not to imagine the outcome if the cavalry had shown up sooner.

"I think what's unfortunate and sad is they announced this was happening, and suddenly people are showing all this support for them," she added. "They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love? People need to put the hate away and show the love, because they could have really used that. I know it was there, but it wasn't as vocal as maybe some of the negativity."

Other members of Digital Extremes have already offered their thoughts on the end of Destiny 2 support. Warframe creative director and Lotus actor Rebecca Ford was one of the first to share appreciation for Bungie's impact, calling Destiny a "force of nature" and adding that "there is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games."

Everett said that the community team has seen comparisons between Warframe and Destiny "almost every single day." The die-hard players who have logged hundreds – if not thousands – of hours into each game will tell you that the two have less in common than many would think. They each have their own strengths and have been appreciated by different players for different reasons.

Now, as the competition dies down, Everett says she would "never say what has happened to Destiny is a good thing." For Digital Extremes, the existence of a competitor as towering as Destiny seems to have only helped Warframe grow.

"I think having games that are similar enough that the communities can come together and talk about both games, and maybe educate people on those games so people go to both, that's not a bad thing," she added. "I've said for forever, I never expect anyone to only play Warframe. You would burn out so fast if you did. So having other games that scratch a similar itch is healthy for our genre. Having Destiny was healthy for us."

For more, you can read what IGN learned when we caught up with the Warframe team to talk more about its latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations. You can also see how Destiny 2 players did their best to crash servers with the launch of Monument of Triumph .

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

items.title: Warframe Developers Express Their Heartbreak Over The End Of Destiny 2
items.summary: 'No one is celebrating that this has happened' says Warframe's community director
items.content:
While the death of online games can often be met with ugly crowing from those who perceive their gaming choices to be tribal commitments, that’s rarely if ever the case from those working in the same industry. That’s demonstrated by the reaction from the team behind Warframe over the sad news of Destiny 2 ‘s demise. As Eurogamer reports , members of Digital Extremes have expressed their sadness and distress over the end of Bungie’s epic franchise.

“It’s heartbreaking. It’s earth-shattering,” Warframe community director Megan Everett told EG when the site spoke to the studio about its reaction to the shocking news that Destiny 2 will no longer be receiving updates . “I never thought I’d ever see the day where I’d read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down,” Everett continued. “No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny , and its players and that story.” Recognizing that people have described Bungie’s live-service game and Warframe as “direct competitors,” Everett argues that competition is healthy.

Eurogamer also points out that soon after the news broke on May 20, Digital Extremes creative director Rebecca Ford posted to X talked about the enormously important part Bungie games had played in her life, adding, “There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games.” She called Destiny “a force of nature, loved and held in the hands of so many people who, for a moment, were part of the biggest thing in gaming,” and described the game as “the only lasting pillar I had to look up to.”

At the age of 16, me and my best friend (who gave me the name 'rebulast'), picked up our midnight release editions of Halo 3, sped home, told our parents it was important, and played more Halo than you could imagine.

There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games.

The “rising tides float all boats” truism is, well, so often true, and that’s very often the case in video games too, but it’s too often not accepted by certain contingents among players. Fans have rallied behind Destiny 2 since the announcement , although clearly too late, and conspiratorial views regarding Marathon have led to ridiculous Steam review-bombing nonsense. Perceived loyalties become threatened, purity is tested among cliques, and it all results in misguided lashing out. But as Warframe ‘s Everett told Eurogamer , “I’ve said for forever, I never expect anyone to only play Warframe . You would burn out so fast if you did. So having other games that scratch a similar itch is healthy for our genre. Having Destiny was healthy for us.”

Don’t miss the latest reviews, news and tips. Sign up for our free newsletter.

I want to play Vault Breaker right now, but have to wait until July 21

Bungie is trying to recalibrate the extraction shooter's economy before it's too late

Players rushed in the moment the final update went live

Bungie is digging out of some unexpected season 2 launch snafus

Bungie's extraction shooter is facing outages on its biggest day since launch

#WeWantDestiny3 was a ceaseless battle cry on the Amazon-owned platform's livestream of the event

items.title: "No one is celebrating" - Warframe devs on Destiny 2's "earth-shattering" death, and why the genre is weaker for it
items.summary: Speaking to Warframe's community director and live ops lead Megan Everett on the recent end-of-service announcement for Destiny 2.
items.content:
Image credit: Digital Extremes Feature by Connor Makar Staff Writer Published on June 22, 2026 31 comments Follow Destiny 2 In case you are unfamiliar with the sci-fi video game space, one of its giants has come to a sudden and tragic end. Destiny 2 - a juggernaut in the genre - will no longer be receiving updates from its developer Bungie. While fans have flocked back to celebrate the title, it is nonetheless the end of an era.

Its conclusion has left many of its players seeking refuge in other digital universes. Colder, calculating minds may see this as an opportunity for its competitors. However, the public-facing Warframe team - arguably working on Destiny's most direct competitor - have been nothing but mournful.

"Truthfully, it's heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down," community director and live ops lead Megan Everett told Eurogamer.

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [Destiny's developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in."

Soon after it was announced that Destiny 2 would be receiving its final update, Warframe's creative director Rebecca Ford wrote on X that "there is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games", pointing to her own history with both Halo and Destiny. She'd later announce an in-game honoria (account title cosmetic) that players could unlock that directly references Destiny , an homage to its peer.

Everett shares this stance. She pointed to the surge of support for Destiny 2 in the wake of its end-of-support announcement, and shared her bittersweet sentiments on the public reception to the game in recent years.

"I think what's unfortunate and sad is they announced this was happening, and suddenly people are showing all this support for them. They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love! People need to put the hate away and show the love, because they could have really used that. I know it was there, but it wasn't as vocal as maybe some of the negativity.

"Overall, I hate to see that happen to them. I have close friends who play Destiny over Warframe, and ain't mad about it. We have friends over there, to see this happen to them is devastating to the industry. No one saw Destiny going away. They had their ups and downs - we've had our ups and downs. People talk about the content drought and the railjack (space missions) - we all have scary days. To live in a reality where they're gone isn't a positive one."

There have been some who have naturally seen this bad news for Bungie as good news for Digital Extremes . Warframe, having only recently released a new update, is building towards its annual Tennocon event where more reveals are planned and excitement around the game is electric.

Even so, Warframe developers have rejected such premises. Ford, in that aforementioned social media post, concluded with the line: "do not offer succor with comparison!" Again, a sentiment shared by Everett.

"I think we're so used to seeing a Warframe and Destiny comparison almost every single day working on the community team. This can be a conversation that's beneficial - seeing two games that are similar in the sci-fi ways, but are so different elsewhere, catering towards two very different crowds," Everett says. "We tried the PvP thing, and we're not a PvP game! If you're coming to Warframe trying to scratch that itch, you're going to have that itch for a long time.

"I think I would never say what has happened to Destiny is a good thing, even though it 'eliminates a competitor'. That is not our mindset at all. I think competition is healthy. I think having games that are similar enough that the communities can come together and talk about both games, and maybe educate people on those games so people go to both, that's not a bad thing. I've said for forever, I never expect anyone to only play Warframe. You would burn out so fast if you did. So having other games that scratch a similar itch is healthy for our genre. Having Destiny was healthy for us."

items.title: Warframe: Wrath & Pride Builds and Review
items.summary: Similar to Sirius & Orion, there are two new heavy scythes that came to Warframe in the newest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations. These heavy scythes are basically two separate weapons, but they only have a few differentiating factors. One is critical-based, and the other is status-based. They are perfectly fine...
items.content:
Similar to Sirius & Orion, there are two new heavy scythes that came to Warframe in the newest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations . These heavy scythes are basically two separate weapons, but they only have a few differentiating factors. One is critical-based, and the other is status-based. They are perfectly fine weapons on their own. But they are not the most exciting new melee weapons. Below will be our recommended builds for both!

Below will be the build for Wrath, which you will need no forma for:

Then, below will be the build for Pride, which you will need only 1 forma for:

Wrath will need the usual setup for critical chance and damage. I went for a combination of status mods that give it armor stripping, shield stripping, and radiation for shenanigans mid-combat. I went for Melee Duplicate to ensure the best damage output it can have.

For Pride, I went for a Galvanized Elementalist and Condition Overload build with Melee Vortex to pull in enemies and stack on the status effects. I went for Gas for the area-of-effect damage. Aside from this, these are simple builds to achieve if you have the above mods.

Wrath is a fun weapon to use for crowds due to the critical damage, chopping down anything in its path. Its unique trait is fun to pull off, too, as you can slam down and gain more critical chance depending on how many enemies you hit to get sweet, sweet orange crits. Pride, on the other hand, is definitely more suited for bosses, as Condition Overload allows for more single-target damage. It is not suited for crowds, and its similar trait of slamming down on enemies to get more status chance just isn’t as helpful.

However, both of these weapons are definitely worth grinding for in the new activities in Warframe . They are very easy to acquire, just like Sirius & Orion .

Disclaimer – Digital Extremes provided Wrath & Pride for usage in this article.

items.title: Warframe: Sirius & Orion Builds and Review
items.summary: Sirius & Orion in Warframe are honestly very fun, but complicated to understand and use properly. Here are our builds and reviews for them!
items.content:
When Digital Extremes revealed the newest warframes , Sirius & Orion, they definitely went whole hog with the approach. As someone who really enjoys Equinox, seeing a different take on a “2-in-1” warframe was exciting. Little did I know that it would be chaotic and, admittedly, fun. But the catch with these 2 is that you will need to build them separately. They function solely on their own stats for their own abilities. Below will be the builds I landed on for them!

And the one below will be for Orion. Both builds require 0 forma:

Let’s start with Sirius. Going for a pure range and duration build works wonders with his abilities. Sirius is going to be used more for his healing circle and the fiery orbs you can summon above him. He and Orion’s first ability, I didn’t get much use out of, however. Both the orbs and healing circle benefit greatly from this combat; do not stress over the power strength with him, keeping it in the positive is good enough. Arcane Energize is a good pick to ensure you and your allies are capped off on energy.

Now for Orion. Orion is suited best for power strength on the flipside of Sirius. His third ability works well with the power strength boost, and you will always want to be Orion when you activate the ultimate to max out the damage output. Molt Augmented for Orion is a must, as he will need all the power strength he can get. Molt Efficiency for both Siurs & Orion gives them enough duration to keep their abilities going for as long as possible.

Now, for the Archon Shards I picked out for them. These are purely for flavor and can be swapped out to suit your playstyles in Warframe:

I also recommend going Unairu for their focus school. This ensures you can strip enemy armor and shields for better damage output.

Sirius & Orion are going to be initially very confusing to use, trust me. But after you get into the swing of things with them? They are a ton of fun. As previously mentioned, their first ability, I did not get a whole lot of use out of. If you want to subsume that with the usual Nourish or Roar, feel free. But Sirius & Orion are the most intricate frames in Warframe to use. The number of use cases for them both makes them very versatile. Whether you are in extermination, mobile defense, or any other activity. You should absolutely grind for these two. Their parts are very easy to get out of the new activities.

Disclaimer – Digital Extremes provided Siurs & Orion for usage in this article.

items.title: Warframe of Mind: Shining some light on Jade Shadows Constellations
items.content:
Yes, I feel like the Warframe developers are being a little overly responsive to memes at the moment. I know that there have been a whole lot of jokes about how the game is for the moms, so now we have an update that’s just All The Dads. Out of nowhere, we get an additional dad on top of existing dad drama, and I half expected for Father to show up even though there’s nothing going on remotely related to Deimos just to have all the dads in one place .

I am being a little snarky, of course, but I do appreciate that this particular update comes with a narrative quest and not just some new gameplay elements, which isn’t always a given. And frankly, I feel like this one had some stakes riding on it compared to other updates to the game. After all, Follie’s update was not precisely received warmly aside from the frame herself. So how are we doing now that we’re following up on a pretty unique storyline?

Warning: While I am not going to spoil the events of the quest directly, I will be referencing the major characters involved and the resolution in oblique terms. This may involve some spoilers along the way. If you haven’t done the quest yet and wish to avoid any spoilers, skip ahead to the first image break.

First and foremost, I appreciate the stuff that this particular update doesn’t bother with. It does not explain how Sirius and Orion grew up or the mechanics of that, nor does it content seriously with the idea of a fully natural Warframe infant in any sense. (This might be explored when the frame gets a Prime; it might not.) It doesn’t even really engage with perhaps the biggest deliberate question mark of how the boys traveled into the past in the first place.

Why are they here? Because they are. We already know that time travel is possible and, in fact, have known this for quite some time. Why does anything happen? Just move past it.

Speaking personally, I had picked Sirius back when I first did the quest, and I suspect that alters some dialogue, but we are spared any period of time in which we treat this like it is an ambiguous or vague scenario. The Stalker needs to protect his son, and just as the current baby is his son, both of these adults are his son. There isn’t a matter of one or the other. He needs to resolve this scenario.

And what a resolution we get. As mentioned above, I am trying to avoid serious spoilers, but not only is one major character implied to be out of commission, it’s at least somewhat possible that two are. (The third… doesn’t seem to be? I feel like the story kind of forgets about our Guest Star Dad a little bit, although given the randomness of his inclusion, this is forgivable.) I hope we get more material following up on the consequences of this change, even just as incidental dialogue in the future.

Now, as in most narrative quests, you wind up being handed a specific Warframe to play the whole time… and unfortunately, it’s the Stalker. I loathe playing as the Stalker. I don’t enjoy Dread or Despair as weapons, which is why I don’t use them. I don’t like playing as Ash (with whom he shares 50% of his abilities). He doesn’t even have some useful new synergies with any of these abilities! He’s just a variant on Ash with fewer words!

The part that makes this somewhat less onerous is, of course, that it makes perfect sense for us to be controlling him. But it also becomes somewhat more onerous insofar as we have less reason than ever to not be helping the Stalker directly. I realize I’m grousing over a comparatively minor thing here, but it does stand out to me, just like the fact that the new game modes offer us two variants on a scythe for the weapon reward.

I mean, given this family, a bow and throwing knives would just make sense, you feel me? Ah well.

Once you’ve finished the quest – which is pretty straightforward but fun, which is about what I hope for from this content – you get access to the new Railjack missions, and I have to admit this makes me grouse a little bit just because… I do actually have a developed Railjack, and I want to be able to use it instead of the Railjack belonging to the NPCs. It’s a fairly minor thing, but it does have an impact, since queueing up with a group means you also don’t get progress toward affinity on your own Railjack. (You do gain points for Intrinsics, though.) The actual missions themselves are nicely extended, though, and I understand why they’re structured like this.

Also, the boss fights are… fairly all right, by Warframe standards! They don’t feel like unfair nonsense but also don’t feel pointlessly easy or meandering. And it would have been easy to make Ryoku and Vena mirrors of one another, but no, each of them have very distinct boss fights and equivalent but not identical missions to fight them.

I am left with a lot of questions about Ryoku and Vena, seeing as how in a strict sense neither of them is “technically” a protoframe (they’re from the future, not the past, and from a time when both Garuda and Ash already exist). There are also loads of questions about them individually, which neither is inclined to answer in any particular detail. Vena has some connection to the Grineer and Kuva, obviously, and at one point she refers to herself being in a romantic relationship with Orion; by contrast, Ryoku is a bit more taciturn, but there’s still some obvious backstory there we don’t know about. And some of it we don’t need to know, but I’m curious!

For that matter, there’s the obvious question about what, exactly, led to their doomed futures. It’s obvious that things unfolded differently in both timeframes, but in both timelines when Sirius or Orion grow up, there appears to be a doom point. What is that point? Does it imply that the Stalker won in these timeline and killed the player? Or is there something else going on?

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack
items.summary: Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warfra
items.content:
Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warframe's latest musical tracks, exquisitely performed and produced in-house by our talented Sound Team.

Where can I listen?

You can find the Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack on:
  • Spotify
  • Apple Music
  • YouTube Music
  • Amazon Music
  • Tidal
  • Deezer
  • Bandcamp
Additionally, we've created a new playlist with help from UNFD, the record label who helped us bring ERRA's "Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven" to our unforgettable Jade Shadows: Constellations animated short. Find Warframe x UNFD on Spotify and listen to a specially-curated assortment of tracks from Warframe, ERRA and other artists.

FULL TRACKLIST:

1. Celestial Clash
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir:Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
2. Sacred Light
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir: Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
  • Koto: Ray Hickey
  • Shakuhachi: Ron Korb
  • Lyrics: Matthew Chalmers
LYRICS: Where sacred light shines The song of life begins As the shadow of time The protector marches evermore towards silence Glory on high Where the sacred light shines

items.title: Our Refreshed Refer a Friend Program
items.summary: Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below. Referral Code Changes You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform sav
items.content:
Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below.

Referral Code Changes

You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform save enabled account! All you need to do is log into your account on Warframe.com, generate a referral code from the Refer a Friend page, then send that code to the friend you are trying to recruit. After your friend creates their Warframe account, they have seven days to qualify as your recruit. Your friend then inputs your referral code and links your accounts together by either:
  • Entering the referral code in-game by hitting "Communication > Friends > Refer Tenno".
  • Entering the referral code on the Account Management page.

Reward Changes

Both recruiters and recruits can earn Rewards through the new Refer a Friend system.

Recruiter Rewards

There are now four Reward tiers for recruiters to earn depending on how many referrals they send! Recruit 1 Tenno
  • 3x Forma
  • Orokin Catalyst
  • Orokin Reactor
  • Exilus Weapon Adapter
  • Exilus Warframe Adapter
  • Dream Catcher Honoria
Recruit 2 Tenno
  • Two Weapon Slots
Recruit 3 Tenno
  • Warframe Slot
Recruit 4 Tenno
  • Two Archwing Weapon Slots
  • Gravimag
Please note: Once your recruit completes "The Teacher" Quest, you will unlock your recruiter reward! The more Tenno you recruit, the more rewards you get! These Rewards are also retroactive for anyone that qualifies.

Recruit Rewards

After recruits successfully link their accounts, they will instantly gain the following in-game rewards:
  • 7-Day Affinity Booster
  • Classic Color Palette
  • Clan Key

Additional Rewards

Every time one of your recruits makes their first Platinum purchase, 10 percent of the value of Platinum they purchased will be automatically added to your account.

items.title: Styanax Prime Access

You can also earn Styanax Prime and his signature Prime Weapons in-game by collecting and cracking Void Relics! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760260/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Weapons_Pack/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78326/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Prime/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78308/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Complete/ https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760270/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Accessories_Pack/ Styanax Prime Access includes:

  • 90-Day Resource Booster
 Note: You'll find the best value in the Styanax Prime Access Complete pack, which includes the Styanax Prime Warframe, his Prime Weapons and Accessories, as well as Platinum!

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Access Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Available Now
items.summary: ***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations! NEW QUEST The consequences continue in
items.content:
***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations!

NEW QUEST

The consequences continue in a new cinematic sequel Quest that picks up where 2024's Jade Shadows left off. Reassume control of the Stalker as he allies once more with the Tenno to navigate a conflict of celestial proportions. His dueling sons from parallel futures have come to the Origin System in search of their father, and will stop at nothing to claim him for their respective timelines. Only you can end their cycle of conflict and bring harmony to the Origin System!

NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & ORION

Dedicated warriors borne of relentless determination. Sirius & Orion comprise the very first dual Warframe, occupying the same slot in your Arsenal while jockeying for position on the battlefield as they rain cosmic destruction upon foes. With their signature Pride and Wrath Scythes you can use brutal heavy attacks to gain Overguard and increased Critical Chance.

Sirius Ability 1: Coronal Ejection

Hurl Sirius' Jade Light infused scythe, dealing Heat Damage while collecting any pickups in its path. Hold to swap to Orion and use Gravitic Slash.

Orion Ability 1: Gravitic Slash

Repel enemies with Orion’s sinister scythe, dealing Slash Damage while reducing shields and armor. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Coronal Ejection.

Sirius Ability 2: Jade Stars

Sirius conjures Jade Light motes that slowly regenerate over time. Attacking enemies launches the motes, dealing Heat Damage. Hold to swap to Orion and use Astral Shell.

Orion Ability 2: Astral Shell

Envelop Orion in an Astral Shell. Upon taking damage the shell becomes a decoy that draws fire until it's destroyed. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Jade Stars.

Sirius Ability 3: Light’s Sanctuary

Sirius creates a well of light that heals and revives allies, while reducing incoming damage. The well slowly grows in size and power. Hold to swap to Orion and use Event Horizon.

Orion Ability 3: Event Horizon

Orion forms a drifting black hole, trapping enemies within its gravity. Hitting the black hole with Gravitic Slash or Coronal Ejection will extend its duration and change its trajectory: Gravitic Slash will direct it away from Orion, and Coronal Ejection will pull it towards Sirius. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Light's Sanctuary.

Sirius & Orion Ability 4: Celestial Clash

Sirius & Orion take to the skies in a cosmic duel. Each attack consumes a Constellation Star to inflict colossal collateral Blast damage. Match the star's color to the Warframes color to gain increased Critical Chance.

NEW PROTOFRAMES: RYOKU AND VENA

Sirius & Orion haven't arrived on their own. Both have trained extensively under the guidance and mentorship of two equally ruthless fighters mired in an intense rivalry of their own. The Ash Protoframe, Ryoku, is a discerning assassin who delivers violence with a disturbing discipline that he has passed along to Sirius. His counterpart, the Garuda Protoframe Vena, indulges her bloodlust with a frenzied brutality befitting her "Wytch" moniker, which she has likewise instilled in Orion.

RETURN TO RAILJACK

Completing the Quest will unlock the Uranus Proxima which includes our most accessible Railjack Missions to date. Here there's no requirement to level up — or even own — a Railjack of your own! Simply enter the Pontis Tower hub, decide whether you'd prefer to fight for Ryoku or Vena, and board their stylized vessels to engage in some of Warframe's most epic space combat yet.

DANTE TYTONIS COLLECTION

Knowledge glides on dark wings with the Dante Tytonis Skin, Noctua Lophos and Gallistrix Warfan Skin. An appropriately avian-themed Collection for the venerable scribe Warframe.

NEW INCARNONS

Five new weapons have been altered by the infernal power of the Void for your destructive pleasure. Wield new Incarnon versions of the Ballistica (Crossbow), Destreza (Rapier), Obex (Sparring), Stug (Pistol), and Vectis (Sniper Rifle), each more indomitable than ever before.

QUALITY OF LIFE IMPROVEMENTS

Nidus Retouch

The avatar of the Infested, Nidus, is more pestilent than ever thanks to an Ability Retouch. Bestowed with significant health boosts and other improvements, this walking plague is both more accessible and enjoyable to wield on the battlefield.

Additional Improvements

As ever, we're improving the overall Warframe experience with several new additions and upgrades, including overhauled lighting in Corpus Ships, adding Damage Over Time (DOT) to enemy Health Bars, enhanced Junction Bosses, Camera offset options, Companion XP adjustments, four new Arcanes and more.

JADE SHADOWS: CONSTELLATIONS PACKS

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735390/Warframe_Constellations_Complete_Collection/ Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations!
